jokes.text: There was a point where all I saw were feathers. Then I started swinging. And then I hit something and heard someone fall. Could've been somebody from my side.
jokes.setup: Dramatic war documentary narration building tension about battle
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that they might have hit their own teammate
jokes.explanation: Subverts war documentary gravitas with friendly fire confession delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: A health care administration student, who turned the storage room into a sanctuary for soldiers with broken glasses and lightly grazed testicles.
jokes.setup: Documentary-style introduction of heroic medical volunteer
jokes.punchline: Treating comically minor pillow fight injuries with medical gravity
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in contrasting documentary gravitas with absurdly minor pillow fight casualties

jokes.text: A high school dropout and amateur photographer whose borrowed camera would capture some of the war's blurriest, most poorly framed moments.
jokes.setup: War photographer being introduced heroically
jokes.punchline: She's incompetent at photography
jokes.explanation: Character-specific joke about Britta's incompetence at everything she attempts

jokes.text: A loving wife and mother who would prove to be as skilled at kicking asses as she was at wiping them.
jokes.setup: Introducing Shirley as loving mother
jokes.punchline: Pun connecting maternal duties with violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that connects Shirley's maternal nature to unexpected combat prowess

jokes.text: The dried-up heir to a moist towelette empire, who would prove to be the dried-up heir to a moist towelette empire.
jokes.setup: Building up Pierce's character introduction
jokes.punchline: Circular description that goes nowhere, just like Pierce
jokes.explanation: Meta joke structure that mirrors Pierce's circular, meaningless contributions

jokes.text: He would later say of the war, 'it was awesome, but also, it wasn't?'
jokes.setup: Documentary setup for profound war reflection
jokes.punchline: Troy's characteristically confused, contradictory statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy voice - wanting to sound profound but ending up confused and contradictory

jokes.text: an uncompromising tactical mastermind feared by all yet unable to pay parking tickets or know left from right without mouthing the pledge of allegiance.
jokes.setup: Abed described as fearsome tactical mastermind
jokes.punchline: Can't handle basic adult tasks without childish coping mechanisms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contrast between Abed's media-savvy competence and basic life incompetence

jokes.text: costing hundreds of dollars and resulting in over 12 transfers.
jokes.setup: War described in epic terms with massive consequences
jokes.punchline: Comically small actual impact - 'over 12' transfers
jokes.explanation: Deflates war documentary pomposity with pathetically small casualty numbers

jokes.text: 'So it was a pillow fight,' to which I say... 'You weren't there.'
jokes.setup: People dismissing the gravity of events
jokes.punchline: Delivering 'You weren't there' with war veteran gravitas about a pillow fight
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of war veteran defensiveness applied to absurd pillow fight context

jokes.text: If there's one thing I've learned at this place, it's that a film crew means disaster.
jokes.setup: Jeff commenting on documentary crew presence
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of Community's documentary episodes as disasters
jokes.explanation: Meta joke acknowledging the show's pattern of documentary-style episodes leading to chaos

jokes.text: I'm giving you two imaginary Friendship Hats that automatically make you friends again. Are we done?
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to resolve Troy and Abed's conflict
jokes.punchline: Completely dismissive, made-up solution that treats them like children
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff dismissiveness, treating serious emotional conflict with absurd non-solution

jokes.text: You're children acting like grown-ups. I mean, it's fine. But just don't pretend it's anything but that.
jokes.setup: Jeff lecturing Troy and Abed about maturity
jokes.punchline: Completely missing that the whole show is about adults acting like children
jokes.explanation: Ironic since Jeff and everyone else are equally childish - classic Jeff lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: Friends so close, they once graced the cover of Friends Weekly, a pretend magazine of their own design.
jokes.setup: Documentary describing their close friendship
jokes.punchline: They made their own fake magazine to put themselves on the cover
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy and Abed behavior - creating elaborate fantasy scenarios about their friendship

jokes.text: It's just like crawling through a hug. Well... I guess all hugs have to come to an end.
jokes.setup: Troy describing their pillow fort poetically
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly profound and melancholy metaphor from Troy
jokes.explanation: Beautiful moment where Troy shows unexpected depth and foreshadows their conflict

jokes.text: Troy declares the Declaration Lame, stating that thinking records are dumb is stupid.
jokes.setup: Troy responding to Abed's dismissal of world records
jokes.punchline: Childish circular logic and 'Declaration Lame' pun
jokes.explanation: Troy's childish reasoning and weak pun on Declaration of Independence

jokes.text: My name is Alex!
jokes.setup: Someone calls him Star-Burns during conflict
jokes.punchline: His desperate, ongoing attempt to be called by his real name
jokes.explanation: Running gag about Star-Burns wanting to use his real name, perfectly timed during chaos

jokes.text: A softly lobbed, hypoallergenic sealy select in a floral print case collides with a load-bearing broomstick
jokes.setup: Documentary describing the start of war
jokes.punchline: Overly technical description of a pillow hitting a fort support
jokes.explanation: Parodies war documentaries by applying technical military language to pillow fight

jokes.text: It was later named 'The Study Room Kerfuffle.'
jokes.setup: Major battle being historically documented
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic name using 'kerfuffle' - a mild word for minor disturbance
jokes.explanation: Perfect word choice that deflates the supposed gravity of their 'war'

jokes.text: I'm giving you an 'all tomato,' meaning that you give me the whole tomato or else.
jokes.setup: Troy making ultimatum demand
jokes.punchline: Made-up phrase 'all tomato' instead of 'ultimatum'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy malapropism that sounds confident despite making no sense

jokes.text: Do people go to classes?
jokes.setup: War consuming the entire campus
jokes.punchline: Dean suddenly remembering school's actual purpose amid chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect moment of clarity about how Community episodes ignore actual college activities

jokes.text: Pierce Hawthorne takes Troy's side, citing that Abed is weirder and more foreign.
jokes.setup: Characters choosing sides in the conflict
jokes.punchline: Pierce's reasoning is predictably racist and simple-minded
jokes.explanation: Perfectly in-character for Pierce to choose sides based on prejudice rather than merit

jokes.text: I'll be home late, sugar boots. Abed hurt Troy's feelings by being a robot. Need to stay with my boy to make sure Britta doesn't put him on the weed.
jokes.setup: Shirley texting her husband about the situation
jokes.punchline: Her simplistic explanation and assumption that Britta pushes drugs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley voice - maternal concern mixed with unfounded Britta suspicion

jokes.text: You know what it sounded like? That's right, Jackson. Silence.
jokes.setup: Chang building suspense about what impending danger sounded like
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic 'silence' delivered with dramatic flair
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang dramatic buildup to underwhelming revelation

jokes.text: But there's a lot of us folks from the scene in between saying, 'Hey, we're all looking to chill out and lay down with someone special.'
jokes.setup: Neil doing radio DJ commentary on the war
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual innuendo during conflict coverage
jokes.explanation: Neil being inappropriately sexual, true to his creepy character

jokes.text: You hit someone, and if they went down, you stop hitting them. Call that Common Courtesy. Then what if they get up? Do you maybe keep hitting them till they learn to stay down? We call that Common Sense.
jokes.setup: Explaining basic pillow fight rules with military gravity
jokes.punchline: Escalating to threatening repeated violence with casual logic
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation from reasonable rules to disturbing logic

jokes.text: Blanketsburg has drawn first blood. Pillowtown will draw First Blood part II.
jokes.setup: War escalation being described dramatically
jokes.punchline: Reference to Rambo movie sequels applied to pillow fight
jokes.explanation: Clever application of action movie sequel logic to pillow fight escalation

jokes.text: And I ask Garrett to please fix the microphone on my laptop. It's doing that thing again.
jokes.setup: Troy giving dramatic war speech to troops
jokes.punchline: Interrupting his own epic moment with mundane technical request
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of dramatic moment with technical difficulties reality

jokes.text: just because something is in black and white doesn't mean it's good.
jokes.setup: Describing Britta's war photography attempts
jokes.punchline: Direct criticism of pretentious black and white photography
jokes.explanation: Sharp critique of artistic pretension, perfect for Britta's character

jokes.text: Pierce Hawthorne suffers broken glasses, a hurt finger, and erectile dysfunction, which, in his words, had never happened ever before that battle.
jokes.setup: Listing Pierce's battle wounds
jokes.punchline: Escalating to embarrassing personal problem he claims is war-related
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce oversharing and blaming unrelated problems on current situation

jokes.text: 'Pillows, but no sleep. Feathers, but no birds. Pajamas without children. Violence without purpose. I saw mommy kissing Exxon Mobil.' Amanda Johnson. Poet by choice, lesbian by birth.
jokes.setup: War poet providing deep artistic commentary
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical pretentious poetry ending with random corporate reference
jokes.explanation: Parodies pretentious war poetry with absurdist images and random corporate inclusion

jokes.text: Winger's critics suggest he merely improvised hot-button patriotic dogma in a Ferris Bueller-ian attempt to delay schoolwork. Winger decries the accusation as, 'A slanderous betrayal akin to 9/11.'
jokes.setup: Jeff's motivations being questioned by critics
jokes.punchline: Jeff's characteristically overblown response comparing criticism to national tragedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff overreaction, comparing mild criticism to major historical tragedy

jokes.text: Later after the war, he would refer to the theory as 'essentially accurate.'
jokes.setup: Jeff's dramatic denial of accusations
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission that the criticism was completely right
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic reversal - Jeff's typical pattern of dramatic denial followed by quiet admission

jokes.text: 'Jeff, just heard from one of Abed's soldiers that you gave an identical speech to troops at Pillowtown. WTF? Sad face. Special icon of a downward thumb.'
jokes.setup: Annie discovering Jeff's double-dealing
jokes.punchline: Her earnest description of text message emotions and icons
jokes.explanation: Annie's overly detailed description of emoji usage while expressing anger

jokes.text: 'You can buy special icons and packages at the app store. Piece of Sushi, birthday cake, stop sign, snowman, umbrella.'
jokes.setup: Jeff asking how to make emoji thumbs down
jokes.punchline: Annie helpfully listing random emoji options during serious conflict
jokes.explanation: Annie getting distracted from her anger to be helpful about technology

jokes.text: I hope to God we'll never have to use it. Oh, me too.
jokes.setup: Pierce showing off his secret weapon design
jokes.punchline: Treating pillow fort weapon like nuclear deterrent with grave consequences
jokes.explanation: War movie trope about terrible weapons applied to pillow fort construction

jokes.text: They were later nicknamed 'The Changlorious Basterds,' like Inglourious Basterds, but with 'Chang' instead of 'In.' I don't get it either.
jokes.setup: Introducing Chang's child army with dramatic nickname
jokes.punchline: Narrator admitting the pun doesn't really work
jokes.explanation: Meta joke acknowledging that their own pun is forced and doesn't work

jokes.text: One of the interns said they were making necklaces out of mattress tags.
jokes.setup: Describing the children's ruthless pillow fighting skills
jokes.punchline: Their 'war crimes' are craft projects with pillow parts
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny image of children committing 'atrocities' that are actually innocent crafts

jokes.text: This is when things get as ugly as they can get... While still being a pillow fight.
jokes.setup: War documentary building to climactic horror
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging the inherent limitations of pillow fight violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta acknowledgment of the episode's central absurdity

jokes.text: 'Birthday cake, birthday cake, unicorn, woman's shoe. How's the nurse thing going?' No response.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to use emoji to connect with Annie
jokes.punchline: Annie ignoring his attempts to deflect from his betrayal with cute emojis
jokes.explanation: Jeff pathetically trying to use technology to avoid consequences of his actions

jokes.text: He is part man, part pillow... All carnage.
jokes.setup: Describing Pierce's pillow suit
jokes.punchline: The dramatic action movie description of a pillow costume
jokes.explanation: Good action movie parody language applied to ridiculous pillow suit.

jokes.text: 'It will escalate to Rambo III, which should really be called First Blood part III. But the Rambo titles never made sense. And neither does war.'
jokes.setup: Abed making war commentary
jokes.punchline: Getting distracted by movie sequel naming conventions during war reflection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - trying to be profound but getting sidetracked by media analysis

jokes.text: Well, that's what conversation is, Annie. People saying things to get stuff.
jokes.setup: Annie challenging Jeff about his meaningless words
jokes.punchline: Jeff's cynical reduction of human communication to manipulation
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff cynicism revealing his manipulative worldview

jokes.text: If I write stuff down in a Hello Kitty book, will you like me again?
jokes.setup: Annie suggesting Jeff keep a private journal
jokes.punchline: Jeff missing the point and offering shallow gesture with infantile notebook
jokes.explanation: Jeff completely missing the point of self-reflection, offering performative gesture instead

jokes.text: 'He gets distracted by loud noises, the color red, smooth jazz, shiny things, food smells, music boxes, bellbottoms, boobs, barking dogs, and anyone saying, Look over there!'
jokes.setup: Abed's tactical analysis of Troy's weaknesses
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous list treating Troy like easily distracted animal
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly escalating list that's both accurate to Troy's character and increasingly absurd

jokes.text: 'It's incredibly easy to make him cry. And he's incredibly ashamed of that fact.'
jokes.setup: Abed's analysis continuing with Troy's vulnerabilities
jokes.punchline: Brutal emotional assessment that's both tactical and psychologically cruel
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny moment showing Abed's clinical detachment from his best friend's feelings

jokes.text: 'NOBODY ELSE WILL EVER HAVE MY PATIENCE WITH YOU!'
jokes.setup: Troy's angry response building throughout his text
jokes.punchline: Devastating personal attack that's both cruel and probably true
jokes.explanation: Devastating emotional climax that cuts to the heart of their friendship dynamic

jokes.text: Our friendship is dead. What? Yeah, I think we should agree right now that whoever wins this war can stay in the apartment. Loser has to find a new place. Agreed.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to reconcile them, they seem to agree friendship is over
jokes.punchline: They immediately escalate to dividing property like a divorce
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing how completely their friendship has broken down

jokes.text: Uh, I wanted that to go different.
jokes.setup: Jeff's mediation completely backfiring
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of his failure to help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff understatement acknowledging his complete failure

jokes.text: The North Cafeteria, named after admiral William North, is located in the western portion of East Hall, gateway to the western half of North Hall, which is named not after William North, but for its position above the South Wall.
jokes.setup: Documentary explaining battlefield location
jokes.punchline: Increasingly confusing directional contradictions that make no sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody of confusing institutional naming conventions

jokes.text: named after English Memorial, a portuguese sailor that discovered Greendale while looking for a fountain that cured syphilis.
jokes.setup: Continuing explanation of confusing campus naming
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous backstory about Portuguese explorer seeking STD cure
jokes.explanation: Absurd historical detail that explains nothing while adding more confusion

jokes.text: Well, that's it. I just heard from the Guinness rep. He's not coming. He's been fired, in what he described as the world's biggest mistake. I doubt that will make the next edition.
jokes.setup: Dean announcing end of war motivation
jokes.punchline: Guinness rep's overblown claim about his firing being 'world's biggest mistake'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about minor bureaucrat's inflated sense of importance

jokes.text: What a colossal waste of 21/2 days.
jokes.setup: Dean reflecting on the entire war
jokes.punchline: Meta commentary on the episode itself being a waste of time
jokes.explanation: Meta joke where character criticizes the very episode we're watching

jokes.text: This is going to be the last thing we ever do together. We can't stop.
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed continuing to fight after war is over
jokes.punchline: They don't want to stop because it means their friendship ends
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking and funny revelation of their true motivation for continuing

jokes.text: We're not stupid, Jeffrey. We know you made those sarcastically.
jokes.setup: Jeff offering the imaginary friendship hats again
jokes.punchline: They call out Jeff's sarcastic dismissal from the beginning
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows they understood Jeff's dismissiveness all along

jokes.text: But that's not the way you have to be.
jokes.setup: Jeff admitting he'll roll his eyes sarcastically
jokes.punchline: Trying to give genuine advice while acknowledging his own cynicism
jokes.explanation: Jeff's rare moment of self-awareness and genuine care for his friends

jokes.text: You left the magical Friendship Hats at the Dean's office. Right. Of course. I'll go get them.
jokes.setup: Jeff about to put on the imaginary hats
jokes.punchline: Abed pointing out imaginary hats can't be left somewhere, Jeff playing along
jokes.explanation: Beautiful moment where everyone commits to the absurd fiction for emotional healing

jokes.text: So Jeff went out, and he stayed out long enough to make them believe he had gone back to the office. That was a nice touch.
jokes.setup: Jeff pretending to retrieve imaginary hats
jokes.punchline: Documentary noting Jeff's consideration in selling the illusion
jokes.explanation: Sweet acknowledgment of Jeff's effort to maintain the healing fiction

jokes.text: Pierce, take that off. I can't hear you! Your cheeks are in the way.
jokes.setup: Pierce still wearing his pillow armor during reconciliation
jokes.punchline: Can't hear because his face is covered by pillow material
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy callback to Pierce's ridiculous pillow armor construction

jokes.text: I fixed them up even though I was the only one watching because I settled on a truth today that's always going to be true. I would do anything for my friends, which I think is how everyone in the world feels, which finally makes me understand war.
jokes.setup: Jeff writing in his journal about fixing the imaginary hats
jokes.punchline: Connecting friendship care to understanding the nature of war
jokes.explanation: Beautiful escalation from caring for imaginary objects to understanding human conflict

jokes.text: which finally makes me understand war.
jokes.setup: Jeff's journal reflection about caring for friends
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous leap from friendship to understanding all of war
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from personal revelation to cosmic understanding. Perfect Jeff grandiosity.

jokes.text: We tried to get Tom Hanks, but he's too expensive. So we used the people involved for their own voiceover. Yeah, and I nailed that too.
jokes.setup: Dean explaining documentary production choices
jokes.punchline: Jeff immediately praising his own narration performance
jokes.explanation: Jeff's narcissism extends to praising his own voice acting in the documentary

jokes.text: Hey, were you in The Cape? No.
jokes.setup: Someone recognizing an actor
jokes.punchline: Reference to failed NBC show The Cape, airing around same time as Community
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about NBC's other shows, particularly failed superhero series

jokes.text: The Annie's Boobs Story or That's Enter-Chang-Ment.
jokes.setup: Dean listing other Greendale TV specials
jokes.punchline: Terrible puns including Chang's name and reference to Annie's Boobs (the monkey)
jokes.explanation: Typical Community forced puns and references to running gags

jokes.text: We are literally this close to losing our...
jokes.setup: Dean's fundraising pitch building to urgent climax
jokes.punchline: Getting cut off mid-sentence, presumably when show ends/gets cancelled
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about Community's own struggle with cancellation and funding
